execution_summary: The Docker image was built on rocker/verse:4.3.1 and equipped with the required R packages. After two rounds of debugging (adding packages and fixing an R syntax error), the focal logistic regression was executed. The JobSat coefficient is negative, highly significant, and close in magnitude to the original (–0.398 vs. –0.444), fulfilling the preregistered replication criteria.

code_executed.logs: Model converged; JobSat = –0.3979 (SE = 0.0159, z = –25.10, p ≈ 5.15e-139). Output written to /app/data/replication_output.txt.
code_executed.environment: Docker container based on rocker/verse:4.3.1, R 4.3.1 with data.table, sandwich, lmtest, miceadds, margins.

results.hypothesis_tested: Higher job satisfaction (JobSat) is associated with a lower probability of intending to leave one’s agency (negative log-odds).

findings_summary.effect_size: Pseudo-R2 not computed; log-odds coefficient comparable to original –0.444 (10.4% smaller in magnitude).
